n8n-io/n8n#14497.body:
### Bug Description

I’ve identified a bug in the OAuth2 PKCE implementation in the n8n credential system. The issue is that even though the target API (Mercado Livre) requires the client_secret, it is not being sent in the final token exchange, leading to authentication failure.

### To Reproduce

Steps to Reproduce the Bug
Create an OAuth2 Credential:

Open your n8n dashboard (version 1.86.0).

Navigate to the credentials section and create a new OAuth2 credential.

Select PKCE as the OAuth Grant Type.

Configure the Credential:

Fill in all required fields, including client_id and client_secret.

Configure the following URLs:

Authorization URL: https://auth.mercadolivre.com.br/authorization

Access Token URL: https://api.mercadolibre.com/oauth/token

Redirect URI: https://<your-domain>/rest/oauth2-credential/callback

Ensure that the Authentication Method is set to Body.

Start the OAuth2 Flow:

Initiate the authentication process via n8n.

You will be redirected to the login page of the API provider (Mercado Livre).

Observe the Failure:

After signing in, you will be redirected back to n8n with a code parameter.

n8n attempts to exchange the code for an access token but fails with an error:

json
Copiar
{
  "error": "invalid_request",
  "message": "the following parameters are required: grant_type, client_id, client_secret, code, redirect_uri. Missing parameters: client_id, client_secret"
}
Inspect the Request:

Open your browser’s developer tools and check the network requests.

You will notice that:

The code_verifier is sent correctly.

The client_id and client_secret are missing in the request payload. In particular, the client_secret appears as __n8n_BLANK_VALUE_xxx in the internal callback URL.

### Expected behavior

Expected Behavior
After the user completes the login and is redirected back to n8n with the authorization code, the final token exchange should include all necessary parameters. This means that the POST request sent to the token endpoint must contain the following fields with the correct values:

grant_type: authorization_code

client_id: the provided client identifier

client_secret: the provided client secret

code: the authorization code received from the API provider

redirect_uri: the configured redirect URI in n8n

code_verifier: the generated code verifier from the PKCE process

With these parameters properly included in the request, the authentication flow should complete successfully, and an access token should be returned without error.

### Operating System

Ubuntu Linux 24.04

### n8n Version

1.86.0

### Node.js Version

18.16.0

### Database

SQLite (default)

### Execution mode

main (default)
n8n-io/n8n#14497.title: Bug Report: OAuth2 PKCE flow ignores client_secret in final token exchange

n8n-io/n8n#18439.body:
### Bug Description

Currently the Salesforce JWT Credential input form is broken and always produces the error "secretOrPrivateKey must be an asymmetric key when using RS256"

The core reason seems to be that the input field for the [PEM certificate](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/blob/1ddb10c3c8e8b985cb337afe6c9997ec622cacef/packages/nodes-base/credentials/SalesforceJwtApi.credentials.ts#L53) is using a regular HTML input field instead of a textarea.

This input field automatically strips all newline characters of the PEM certificate. The newline characters are however needed for a valid PEM certificate.

#### Suggested Solution:
Use a text area field for the private key to prevent the stripping of newline characters. Alternatively, it would be even better UX if users could select the key file via a file selector.

### To Reproduce

1. Go to credentials tab of your project
2. Click "Create Credential"
3. Select "Salesforce JWT API"
4. Fill in valid information in the form. Most importantly copy the content of a valid key in PEM format into the "Private Key" field.

In order to test if this really is the issue, I manipulated the edit request to send the credentials in the correct format to the server, which did fix the issue. To try this:
1. Open the network inspection tab in your browser
2. Edit an existing "Salesforce JWT API" credential to record the request to the server
  - It will be a PATCH request to *.app.n8n.cloud/rest/credentials/<credential-id>
3. Right-Click the request and select "Edit and Resend" or similar
4. Change the `data.privateKey` value in the body to contain proper newline characters (`\n`)
5. Send the edited request
6. Trigger the test endpoint again to see a successful test

### Expected behavior

The expected behavior is that the key in PEM format gets stored on the server with the newline characters.

n8n-io/n8n#24185.body:
## Summary

Today, if we're resolving a filepath and the file doesn't exist, `fsRealpath` throws an error. In this case we fall back to `resolve`. This lets us handle the case where we want to create the file eventually e.g., if we're going to `write`. However, `resolve` doesn't actually resolve symlinks. This defeats part of the purpose of our `resolvePath` helper, which is to ensure that we're working with fully resolved, canonical path. (Since this is what we'll check against allow/block lists.)

This change introduces a strategy where:
- If the file doesn't exist, get the realpath of the containing directory. If *that* doesn't exist, then we return "file not found" (since the eventual write operation would fail anyway). If it _does_ exist, we append the basename and use that as our path. Note that we don't have to worry about symlinks in the "last hop" because we read/write with NO_FOLLOW.

Tested manually to verify that we can still write files that didn't previously exist. Also verified that symlinks end up getting resolved correctly.

## Related Linear tickets, Github issues, and Community forum posts

https://linear.app/n8n/issue/CAT-1852

## Review / Merge checklist

- [ ] PR title and summary are descriptive. ([conventions](../blob/master/.github/pull_request_title_conventions.md)) <!--
   **Remember, the title automatically goes into the changelog.
   Use `(no-changelog)` otherwise.**
-->
- [ ] [Docs updated](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n-docs) or follow-up ticket created.
- [ ] Tests included. <!--
   A bug is not considered fixed, unless a test is added to prevent it from happening again.
   A feature is not complete without tests.
-->
- [ ] PR Labeled with `release/backport` (if the PR is an urgent fix that needs to be backported)

### Bug Description

## Summary

<!--
Describe what the PR does and how to test.
Photos and videos are recommended.
-->
Adds HMAC-SHA256 signature verification for incoming webhook requests in the `ZendeskTrigger` node, ensuring only authentic requests from Zendesk are processed.

Solution based on the **[GitHub Node](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/24203)**.

### Changes
- Verify `X-Zendesk-Webhook-Signature` HMAC header before processing webhook requests
- Fetch signing secret from Zendesk API (`GET /webhooks/{id}/signing_secret`) after webhook creation
- Store signing secret in workflow static data for signature verification
- Return 401 Unauthorized for invalid/missing signatures
- Fix: delete `webhookId` instead of `triggerId` in cleanup

### Implementation Details
- Uses HMAC-SHA256 with base64 encoding per Zendesk's specification
- Constant-time comparison (`timingSafeEqual`) to prevent timing attacks
- Backwards compatible: existing workflows without stored secrets continue to work

n8n-io/n8n#25186.body:
## Summary                                              
                                                       
  Fixes a bug where sidebar menu labels (Home,         
  Personal, Shared, etc.) disappeared after refreshing 
  the page, showing only icons.                        
                                                       
  ### Root Cause:                                          
  In commit 83bf5e16ec (#25096), the sidebar collapse  
  state was changed to default to true (collapsed) when
   the value is null:                                  
                                                       
  // Before:                                           
  const isCollapsed = computed(() =>                   
  uiStore.sidebarMenuCollapsed);                       
                                                       
  // After (buggy):                                    
  const isCollapsed = computed(() =>                   
  uiStore.sidebarMenuCollapsed ?? true);               
                                                       
  When the page loads, sidebarMenuCollapsed is         
  initially null before the SIDEBAR_EXPANDED_EXPERIMENT
   logic runs. This caused the sidebar to render in    
  collapsed mode (icons only, no labels) before the    
  experiment could determine the user's variant.       
                                                       
  ### The Fix:                                             
  Changed the default from true to false, so the       
  sidebar defaults to expanded mode (showing labels)   
  when the state is null:                              
                                                       
  const isCollapsed = computed(() =>                   
  uiStore.sidebarMenuCollapsed ?? false);              
                                                       
  This ensures labels are visible by default, providing
   a better initial experience while the A/B experiment
   determines the final state.

## Summary

Today we came across a failing migration on a crash-looping cloud instance:
```
Starting migration ChangeValueTypesForInsights1759399811000                      
Migration "ChangeValueTypesForInsights1759399811000" failed, error: SQLITE_ERROR: near "10": syntax error
```

In sqlite, OFFSET needs to come after LIMIT, otherwise the statement will fail.

This only affects 2.7.x. Regression from https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/25044

The CI for our migrations runs in multi-main mode with postgres, which is likely why this wasn't caught before merging.

Use when the user provides a message from Telegram and wants to convert it into structured task JSON with main tasks and subtasks. Also use when the user asks to parse airdrop instructions, quest requirements, or any message containing actionable steps that need to be organized hierarchically. --- # Telegram Task Extractor ## Overview Parse Telegram messages (typically crypto airdrop and quest announcements) and extract structured tasks with intelligent hierarchy detection. Output formatted JSON for n8n workflow consumption. ## Strict Extraction Rules **Follow these rules precisely:** 1. **Extract only what is clearly written** - Never invent, guess, or paraphrase information 2. **If no value is found, keep it empty** - Don't fill in missing information 3. **Never expand or elaborate** - Copy exact wording from message when possible 4. **Task descriptions**: Only include if explicitly stated in the message; leave `""` if not clear 5. **Output format**: JSON only, no explanations, no extra text, no commentary outside the JSON structure ## Core Extraction Rules ### Task Detection Philosophy **A task is an actionable item requiring human execution.** Identify actions someone must perform: - ✅ Claim faucet - ✅ Register domain - ✅ Deploy smart contract - ✅ Verify wallet - ✅ Submit form - ✅ Join testnet - ✅ Add network to wallet and claim faucet - ✅ Do transaction on testnet **Ignore:** - ❌ Completed actions - ❌ Questions without action - ❌ General information or announcements - ❌ Context or background details ### 1. Project Name Detection - **If provided in context** (e.g., from n8n workflow variable), use that value directly - **If extracting from message**: Use the message title/header (e.g., "📣 Codex PBC Airdrop" → "Codex PBC") - Include quest/event names when present (e.g., "Codex Christmas Quest III: Mascot Edition") - If no clear project name exists, use the first significant entity mentioned ### 2. Main Task Creation - The main task title should be the quest/event name if present (e.g., "Codex Christmas Quest III: Mascot Edition") - If no specific quest name, use format: "Complete [Project Name] requirements" - Main task's `task_description` should summarize the overall goal and rewards - Main task inherits the due date and priority of the entire quest ### 3. Subtask Extraction **Critical: Group related actions together. Do NOT over-fragment into micro-tasks.** - **Platform-specific actions**: Keep all actions on the same platform as ONE subtask - Example: All Twitter/X activities → "Complete Twitter requirements" (include all steps in description) - Example: All wallet actions → "Set up wallet" (include network addition, faucet claim in description) - **Related workflow steps**: Group sequential steps that form a logical unit - Example: "Register domain and deploy contract" vs separate "Register domain" + "Deploy contract" - Subtask `task` field: Broader action covering the complete workflow step - Subtask `task_description`: **Extract only what is explicitly stated in the message** - Never invent, paraphrase, or expand beyond what's written - If nothing specific is stated, leave empty `""` - All subtasks inherit the same `due_date` as the main task unless explicitly different - All subtasks inherit the same `priority` as the main task **Examples of proper grouping:** - ❌ Bad: Separate subtasks for "Post on X", "Use #CodexMascot", "Share mascot" - ✅ Good: One subtask "Complete Twitter promotion" with description listing all steps - ❌ Bad: Separate "Add network", "Claim faucet", "Check balance" - ✅ Good: One subtask "Set up wallet and claim faucet" with description covering all steps ### 4. Date Extraction - Parse natural language dates ("January 4", "26th Dec", "Last Date - 26th Dec") - Format strictly as: `yyyy-MM-dd` - Use today's date ({{ $now.format('yyyy-MM-dd') }}) to infer the correct year - **Default behavior**: If no date is explicitly mentioned in the message, set due date to **2 days from today** - Only leave empty `""` if explicitly told not to set a default ### 5. Priority Detection **Default priority is "Medium"** unless urgency markers are present. Analyze urgency indicators and set accordingly: - **High**: "must", "required", "Important", "Urgent", "ASAP", "before deadline", "immediately", "or you will lose eligibility", "ends soon", "last chance", "within 24 hours", "limited spots", "Deadline", nearby dates (within 7 days) - **Medium**: Standard announcements, moderate timeframes (7-30 days), default for most tasks - **Low**: Long timeframes (30+ days), optional tasks, low-value rewards, informational updates ### 6. Link Extraction - Extract ALL URLs from the message - Place in `important_links` array for both main task and relevant subtasks - If a link is specific to a subtask (e.g., form submission URL), include it in that subtask's `important_links` - If a link is general (e.g., app dashboard), include in main task's `important_links` ## Output Format ```json { "project_name": "Project Name", "alltasks": [ { "task": "Main task title (quest/event name)", "task_description": "Overall goal and rewards summary", "due_date": "yyyy-MM-dd", "priority": "High|Medium|Low", "important_links": ["url1", "url2"], "subtasks": [ { "task": "Subtask action", "task_description": "Relevant details", "due_date": "yyyy-MM-dd", "priority": "High|Medium|Low", "important_links": ["url3"] } ] } ] } ``` ## Language Handling - Support both English and Hindi mixed messages - Extract tasks from both languages - Translate Hindi task descriptions to English for consistency - Common Hindi terms: - "Agar" = If - "lena" = take/get - "karna" = do/make - "check kar lena" = check it ## Examples ### Example 1: Quest with Steps **Input Message:** ``` 📣 Codex PBC Airdrop - Codex Christmas Quest III: Mascot Edition🎄 Rewards: Gilded Role (Top 20 entries), Blueprint Role for other qualified entries Deadline is January 4. ⬇️ How to Participate: 1. Pick Creature - Choose the type of creature your mascot will be 2. Create Mascot - Design your mascot as a static image or animation 3. Post on X - Share your mascot and tag with #CodexMascot 4. Submit Form - https://forms.google.com/submit 🧑‍⚖️ Judging: Based on content quality and engagement ``` **Output:** ```json { "project_name": "Codex PBC", "alltasks": [ { "task": "Codex Christmas Quest III: Mascot Edition", "task_description": "", "due_date": "2025-01-04", "priority": "High", "important_links": ["https://forms.google.com/submit"], "subtasks": [ { "task": "Design and create mascot", "task_description": "Pick creature type (heroic, mythic, mechanical, abstract, or normal animal). Design mascot as static image or animation with strong visual identity.", "due_date": "2025-01-04", "priority": "High", "important_links": [] }, { "task": "Complete Twitter promotion", "task_description": "Share mascot on X and tag with #CodexMascot", "due_date": "2025-01-04", "priority": "High", "important_links": [] }, { "task": "Submit form", "task_description": "", "due_date": "2025-01-04", "priority": "High", "important_links": ["https://forms.google.com/submit"] } ] } ] } ``` **Note**: Steps 1-2 (Pick + Create) are grouped into one subtask since they're part of the same creative process. Twitter actions stay as one subtask. Form submission is separate since it's a distinct action. ### Example 2: Simple Update with Mixed Language **Input Message:** ``` 📣 Lighter Airdrop : Important Update Before TGE If your wallet was hacked, add a new wallet: https://app.lighter.xyz/points Note: Agar Wallet Hack ho gya tha to new wallet add karke Airdrop le sakte ho Last Date - 26th Dec ``` **Output:** ```json { "project_name": "Lighter", "alltasks": [ { "task": "Update wallet address before TGE", "task_description": "", "due_date": "2024-12-26", "priority": "High", "important_links": ["https://app.lighter.xyz/points"], "subtasks": [ { "task": "Add new wallet and create account", "task_description": "Add new wallet in airdrop section. Make sure to create account on Lighter with new wallet.", "due_date": "2024-12-26", "priority": "High", "important_links": ["https://app.lighter.xyz/points"] } ] } ] } ``` **Note**: Wallet-related actions are grouped into one subtask since they're part of the same process. Priority is "High" due to "Important" keyword and "before TGE" urgency. ## Edge Cases ### Multiple Quests in One Message - Create separate main tasks for each distinct quest/airdrop - Each gets its own entry in `alltasks[]` ### No Clear Steps - If message is purely informational with no actionable steps, create a single main task with no subtasks - Task should be "Review [Project Name] announcement" ### Ambiguous Dates - "End of month" → Use last day of current/next month based on context - "Soon", "TBA", or no date mentioned → **Use 2 days from today as default** - Only leave date empty `""` if explicitly instructed not to set a default ### Complex Multi-Step Workflows - Group related steps as subtasks under logical parent tasks - Example: "Connect wallet, claim tokens, stake tokens" → All subtasks under "Complete token claim process" ## Final Output Requirements When responding to task extraction requests: 1. **Output JSON only** - No explanations, commentary, or text outside the JSON structure 2. **No formatting** - No markdown code blocks, no extra whitespace 3. **Complete structure** - Include all required fields even if empty 4. **Valid JSON** - Ensure proper syntax, escaping, and formatting The response should contain ONLY the JSON object starting with `{` and ending with `}`. ### Expected behavior I would execept a clear JSON, output that I give in the example in the system prompt ### Debug Info # Debug info ## core - n8nVersion: 2.6.4 - platform: docker (self-hosted) - nodeJsVersion: 22.22.0 - nodeEnv: production - database: sqlite - executionMode: regular - concurrency: -1 - license: community - consumerId: unknown ## storage - success: all - error: all - progress: false - manual: true - binaryMode: filesystem ## pruning - enabled: true - maxAge: 336 hours - maxCount: 10000 executions ## client - userAgent: mozilla/5.0 (windows nt 10.0; win64; x64) applewebkit/537.36 (khtml, like gecko) chrome/144.0.0.0 safari/537.36 - isTouchDevice: false Generated at: 2026-02-10T09:56:39.149Z ### Operating System Windows ### n8n Version 2.6.4 ### Node.js Version 22.22.0 ### Database SQLite (default) ### Execution mode main (default) ### Hosting self hosted

n8n-io/n8n#25726.body:
## Summary

Addresses several issues found in the last eval run for the AI workflow builder and workflow SDK:

- **Fix CodeBuilder parse failures and think-loop** — Add string concatenation support to AST interpreter (+ with string operands was silently producing NaN), add `.output()`/`.input()` methods on WorkflowBuilder that throw clear error messages, and add a think-loop circuit breaker that forces action after 3 consecutive think-only iterations
- **Handle InputTarget in WorkflowBuilder.to()** — Fix `.to()` to handle InputTarget objects (e.g., `mergeNode.input(0)`), which previously produced broken connections with `node: undefined`. Also deduplicate connections in the InputTarget path
- **Allow constrained property assignment in AST interpreter** — Support simple property assignment (`obj.prop = value`) for subnode dependency ordering, while blocking compound operators and dangerous properties (`__proto__`, `prototype`, `constructor`)
- **Add string composition examples to expression reference** — Prevent the LLM from falling back to JS string concatenation patterns by adding explicit `{{ }}` delimiter examples to the prompt
- **Fix shared subnodes lost when parent nodes have duplicate names** — Auto-renamed nodes were skipping subnode processing and using the original name instead of the resolved map key
- **Auto-rename subnode SDK function names used as variables** — When the LLM generates code like `const embeddings = embedding({...})`, auto-rename the variable instead of throwing a SecurityError
- **Stop validate_workflow loop on empty workflow** — Return `workflowReady: true` when the agent validates with no code, preventing ~50 retries until MAX_AGENT_ITERATIONS
- **Deduplicate drainGenerator helper in tool-dispatch-handler tests** — Minor test refactor
- Remove thinking from workflow

n8n-io/n8n#25945.body:
## Summary
This PR is to fix the non-working EKS Pod Identity credentials resolver when using the system credentials option with AWS Credentials.

#21584 introduced support for the `AWS_CONTAINER_AUTHORIZATION_TOKEN_FILE` required for EKS Pod Identity Authentication. There is an existing bug which predates #21584 which erroneously adds 'Bearer ' as a prefix to the Authorization header. When the EKS Pod Identity Agent makes the AssumeRoleForPodIdentity API call, it expects to find just the token in the Authorization header and passes the value of the header direct to the API as the ServiceAccountToken part of the request.

When a 'Bearer ' prefix is added to the Authorization header, this is also passed to the API call and results in the following error:

`Error fetching credentials: Service account token cannot be parsed: token is malformed: could not base64 decode header: illegal base64 data at input byte 6`

This PR removes the Bearer prefix from the Authorization header when making a call to resolve Pod Identity credentials only. The Bearer prefix is still present when resolving credentials from container metadata for ECS/Fargate.

## Related links
[EKS Pod Identity Usage of the header](https://github.com/aws/eks-pod-identity-agent/blob/2134e9093c2577e2560b96881a997eb9e18d6a25/pkg/handlers/eks_credential_handler.go#L78)

n8n-io/n8n#26007.body:
## Summary

In queue mode, the invike tool method only supported tool nodes with a native `supplyData` method (e.g. `@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.toolHttpRequest`). 

Tool wrapper nodes like`httpRequestTool` and `dataTableTool`, which are normalr nodes marked `usableAsTool` that get their description modified at runtime via `convertNodeToAiTool()` don't have `supplyData`. They use their `execute` method instead.

This adds a path in `invokeTool()` that calls `execute` directly for these nodes when `supplyData` is not available but the node has `AiTool` output, mirroring the existing behavior in `get-input-connection-data.ts`.

**How to test:** Set up an MCP Server Trigger with an HTTP Request Tool node in queue mode. Tool calls should now succeed instead of failing with "does not have supplyData method".

## Related Linear tickets, Github issues, and Community forum posts

https://linear.app/n8n/issue/AI-2074

### Bug Description

After installing npm package of n8n in Windows 10 I have created an Workflow which contains a Python code node. This node repeated giving me error that - "Python runner unavailable: Virtual environment is missing from this system...". Further investigation reveals that - 
1. In task-runner-process-py.js file '.venv/bin/python' path is hardcoded which is a LINUX path. 
2. The path '@n8n/task-runner-python' is missing from the npm installation but this is used in the JS file.

As a result Python script is not running in the workflow.

### To Reproduce

1. Install n8n npm package for Windows
2. run n8n server
3. Create a workflow with a Python code node.
4. Try to run a test run of the default Python code.

### Expected behavior

Python script should run properly without any error like - "Python runner unavailable: Virtual environment is missing from this system
Internal mode is intended only for debugging."

### Debug Info

1. In command prompt run the command - 
type "C:\Users\<username>\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\n8n\dist\task-runners\task-runner-process-py.js"
See the hardcoded paths mentioned in "Bug Description".

### Operating System

Windows 10

### n8n Version

2.9.2

### Node.js Version

24.11.1

### Database

SQLite (default)

### Execution mode

main (default)

### Hosting

self hosted

n8n-io/n8n#26491.body:
### Bug Description

When a workflow has more than one active trigger — for example, a Chat Trigger along with an Execute Workflow Trigger or a Click Trigger — the Chat Trigger stops working properly.

In the editor, when simulating sending messages through the Chat Trigger (using the chat panel in the editor), the following error appears:

`The requested webhook "<workflowId>/<webhookId>" is not registered.`


Additionally, the editor log repeatedly shows:

```
Error handling CollaborationService push message
invalid input syntax for type uuid: "<some_value>"
```


This bug only occurs when there are multiple triggers in the same workflow.
When removing the other triggers and leaving only the Chat Trigger, it starts working normally again.

### To Reproduce

1. Create a workflow in n8n version 2.10.2
2. Add more than one trigger, including a Chat Trigger
3. Activate the workflow
4. Open the workflow in the editor
5. Simulate a chat message in the Chat Trigger panel
6. Observe that the Chat Trigger does not fire rightly and errors appear both in the log and in the editor UI

### Expected behavior

- The Chat Trigger should work normally even when other triggers are active in the workflow.
- No CollaborationService or webhook not registered errors should appear in the log or in the editor interface.

### Debug Info

- Example of log output:

Error handling CollaborationService push message

Error: Error handling CollaborationService push message

    at Push.<anonymous> (/usr/local/lib/node_modules/n8n/src/collaboration/collaboration.service.ts:44:6)

    at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:103:5)

invalid input syntax for type uuid: "fqprawupjv"

___

In "open chat" on workflow editor:

The requested webhook "<workflowId>/<webhookId>" is not registered.


### Operating System

Ubuntu Linux 22.04

### n8n Version

2.10.2

### Node.js Version

24.0.0

### Database

PostgreSQL

### Execution mode

queue

### Hosting

self hosted

n8n-io/n8n#26509.body:
### Bug Description

Users of type `Owner` and `Admin` cannot unpublish workflows anymore, unless these workflows are shared with them. This is important to us: When there are misbehaving workflows, an admin must be able to quickly disable them.

We are aware that workflows can be unpublished using the CLI, but this requires a subsequent n8n restart which has too big an impact.

This affects the editor UI as well as the REST API (API is more important to us).

This used to work and probably broke some time after version 2.6.2.

### To Reproduce

1.  Create an API token in the `Owner` user’s account.
2.  Try to unpublish some workflow (must not be shared with Owner):

        curl -s -H 'X-N8N-API-KEY: ...' -X POST https://n8n.foo.bar/api/v1/workflows/eGNuTTXiW8Xu7wS3/deactivate

3.  Response:

        HTTP/2 403
        server: nginx
        date: Wed, 04 Mar 2026 07:29:35 GMT
        content-type: application/json; charset=utf-8
        content-length: 23
        vary: Accept-Encoding
        etag: W/"17-bqIm6pxC4cx+ZoszvXxsClwgWw8"
        vary: Accept-Encoding

        {"message":"Forbidden"}


### Expected behavior

Workflow should be unpublished/deactivated.

With managed Postgres installations like Supabase for Azure PSQL we have been encountering issues with the recent AI WFB persistence migration.

n8n-io/n8n#26637.body:
Describe the problem/error/question
The Anthropic credential test fails with a 404 error even when using a valid API key. The credential test in AnthropicApi.credentials.js hardcodes claude-3-haiku-20240307 as the test model, but this model is no longer available on newer Anthropic accounts.
What is the error message (if any)?

The resource you are requesting could not be found

Please share your workflow/screenshots/recording
No workflow required — this occurs at the credential setup stage before any workflow is created.
Share the output returned by the last node
N/A — failure occurs during credential verification, not workflow execution.
The hardcoded model in /node_modules/@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain/dist/credentials/AnthropicApi.credentials.js:
json{
  "model": "claude-3-haiku-20240307"
}
Confirmed via curl https://api.anthropic.com/v1/models that this model is not present on the account. Available models start from claude-haiku-4-5-20251001.
Workaround: Manually patching the file to use claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 resolves the issue, but this is overwritten on every n8n update.
Suggested fix: Either update the hardcoded model to a current one, or dynamically query /v1/models and use the first available model for the test request.

n8n version: 2.10.3
Node.js version: 22.22.1

# Original description

## Summary

Hardens the `sanitizeCustomCss` function to prevent style-context breakout when rendering user-provided CSS in form templates.

**Changes:**
- Remove the `textFilter` callback that decoded all HTML entities after tag stripping
- Selectively restore only `>` and `&` post-sanitization (needed for CSS child combinators and values), with a negative lookahead to prevent cascading decode of double-encoded entities
- `<` is never decoded — it is not used in valid CSS
- Add 14 comprehensive regression tests for CSS sanitization

**How to test:**
1. Create a Form Trigger workflow with custom CSS styling containing CSS child selectors (e.g., `div > p { color: red; }`) — verify styling applies correctly
2. Run the new test suite: `cd packages/nodes-base && pnpm test sanitizeCustomCss.test`

https://linear.app/n8n/issue/NODE-4552

n8n-io/n8n#26692.body: ### Bug Description I encountered a situation where unpublished changes in a workflow don’t take effect during production execution if a cron job is used. However, if an error trigger node is used, the production execution runs with the latest version of the workflow, even with unpublished changes. To clarify: When using a cron job trigger, the changes that I make in the workflow, but haven’t published, do not reflect in the production execution. On the other hand, when using an error trigger node, even though the changes are not published, they are still used in the production execution. This seems like an inconsistency in how the workflow versions are applied, depending on the trigger used. I’ve attached images showing both cases for further clarification. ### To Reproduce 1. Create a new workflow or open an existing one. 2. Make changes to a node (for example, add or modify a node) but do not publish the workflow. 3. Use a Cron Trigger node in the workflow and activate the workflow (e.g., set it to run automatically at scheduled intervals). 4. Observe that the changes you made (but did not publish) are not reflected in the production execution. The workflow runs with the previous (published) version. 5. Next, use an Error Trigger node instead of the Cron Trigger node in the same workflow. 6. Activate the workflow again and observe that the unpublished changes are applied in the production execution, even though the workflow was not published. ### Expected behavior https://docs.n8n.io/workflows/publish/ ### Debug Info # Debug info ## core - n8nVersion: 2.9.4 - platform: docker (self-hosted) - nodeJsVersion: 24.13.1 - nodeEnv: production - database: postgres - executionMode: scaling (single-main) - concurrency: -1 - license: enterprise (production) - consumerId: XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX ## storage - success: all - error: all - progress: true - manual: true - binaryMode: database ## pruning - enabled: true - maxAge: 336 hours - maxCount: 150000 executions ## client - userAgent: mozilla/5.0 (windows nt 10.0; win64; x64) applewebkit/537.36 (khtml, like gecko) chrome/145.0.0.0 safari/537.36 - isTouchDevice: false Generated at: 2026-03-06T15:36:29.044Z ### Operating System docker (self-hosted) ### n8n Version 2.9.4 ### Node.js Version 24.13.1 ### Database PostgreSQL ### Execution mode main (default) ### Hosting self hosted

# Description
Backport of #26628 to `release-candidate/2.12.x`.

After this PR has been merged, it will be picked up in the next patch release for release track.

# Original description

## Summary

Problem: import:entities failed with "unexpected EOF" on large ZIP archives in Kubernetes.

Root causes:
1. entities.zip was included in its own archive during export. When import extracted the 0-byte self-referential entry, it truncated the source file mid-read.
2. fflate.Zip (streaming writer) writes 0-size placeholders in local file headers. fflate.Unzip (streaming reader) relies on those headers for file boundaries, causing DEFLATE misalignment on any archive created this way.

Fix: Replace fflate.Unzip in decompressFolder with a custom parser that reads the ZIP central directory — located at the end of the archive, it always contains the correct sizes and offsets regardless of how the archive was created.

Each file is then streamed through Node's built-in zlib using positioned reads, keeping memory usage constant. The source archive path is skipped if it appears as an entry.

## Related Linear tickets, Github issues, and Community forum posts

https://linear.app/n8n/issue/CP-2510/n8n-import-cli-failing-on-k8s

# Description

# Original description

## Summary

Problem: import:entities failed with "unexpected EOF" on large ZIP archives in Kubernetes.

Root causes:
1. entities.zip was included in its own archive during export. When import extracted the 0-byte self-referential entry, it truncated the source file mid-read.
2. fflate.Zip (streaming writer) writes 0-size placeholders in local file headers. fflate.Unzip (streaming reader) relies on those headers for file boundaries, causing DEFLATE misalignment on any archive created this way.

Fix: Replace fflate.Unzip in decompressFolder with a custom parser that reads the ZIP central directory — located at the end of the archive, it always contains the correct sizes and offsets regardless of how the archive was created.

Each file is then streamed through Node's built-in zlib using positioned reads, keeping memory usage constant. The source archive path is skipped if it appears as an entry.

# Original description

## Summary

This fix addresses intermittent task runner connection failures by validating authentication **before** the WebSocket upgrade, preventing opaque close frames when auth fails. The reason why it hang was that the connection was closed by sending a HTTP response **after** the connection had already been upgraded. This meant the other end was expecting WS frames instead of HTTP, which meant the `error` handler attached to `ws` never fired and the connection wasn't closed. This also adds a logic to shutdown the runner if the connection drops since it can't be recovered due to single use token.

**Key changes:**
- Validate grant tokens during the HTTP upgrade request phase (401/403 errors are now returned before upgrade)
- Exit process on unexpected connection close, but skip exit during graceful shutdown
- Improved error logging and test coverage for authentication and connection lifecycle

n8n-io/n8n#27017.body:
## Summary

Hint to reviewer: This PR is deployed to two test instances (for positive test) as well as one test instance with the latest state of 1.x (for negative test).
See this slack message for more details: https://n8nio.slack.com/archives/C0A9PM073QW/p1773822807520939

This PR targets 1.x only, it is not needed for 2.x.

Fixes a regression on the environments feature introduced in https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/pull/21202/ (v 1.122.2)

Video demo of the unexpected behaviour: https://www.loom.com/share/4e3efc2aa6934399bb2d4ffe4b80a83c

In the 1.x world, where a workflow can either by active or inactive, pulling a new version of an active workflow used to have that new version be the new active one.
PR #21202 (linked above) resulted in the new version being pulled, but the old version of the workflow being the one that was still active. As a 1.x user, I have no easy way to detect that my active workflow is actually using an older version for production executions.

The only known workaround is to deactivate and activate an active workflow after pulling.

**For a follow-up:** Should we build the means to inform admins of n8n instances using versions 1.122.2 to 1.123.25 that they have active workflows where the active version is not the latest version of the workflow, and provide them with an API to set the `activeVersionId` of all diverged active workflows to the latest version of each workflow?


## Related Linear tickets, Github issues, and Community forum posts

https://linear.app/n8n/issue/LIGO-331/be-environments-pulled-active-workflow-on-1x-will-keep-executing-older

# Description
Backport of #27126 to `release-candidate/2.13.x`.

## Checklist for the author (@ivov) to go through.

- [ ] Review the backport changes
- [ ] Fix possible conflicts
- [ ] Merge to target branch

After this PR has been merged, it will be picked up in the next patch release for release track.

# Original description



## Summary

Scenario:

1. A main is the leader, has `WaitTracker` running
2. This main terminates, and the key still has this main's name, with a few seconds of TTL left
3. This main restarts and tries to claim leadership via `MultiMainSetup.init` during startup. This fails (as expected) because the key is still set, left over from before the restart. This main marks itself as follower
4. `WaitTracker.init` runs during startup. This main is a follower so `WaitTracker` does not start (as expected)
5. The first `MultiMainSetup.checkLeader` runs. This main reads the key and finds its own name left over from before the restart, and marks itself as leader. But importantly, this main does not fire the `leader-takeover` event, because we're assuming that when a main finds its own name in Redis, this is just a routine leadership check
6. Main is now the leader with no `WaitTracker` so waiting executions do not resume

Likely a `StatefulSet` rolling restart on cloud is faster than a restart in the old docker compose setup on internal, and also the hostname is fixed in k8s vs. dynamic in docker compose, where the hostname defaults to the container ID which changes on restart. 

This fix moves `markAsLeader()` and adds `emit('leader-takeover')` inside the existing guard, so the event fires exactly once on the follower-to-leader transition.

## Related Linear tickets, Github issues, and Community forum posts

https://linear.app/n8n/issue/CAT-2622

### Bug Description

I have maybe a dozen data tables.  Recently when I've tried to download the csv for a table, I always get the same csv, no matter which data table I select for download.

The table data that gets returned every time does happen to be the oldest table, which seems notable.

I've tried using multiple browsers, same issue.

### To Reproduce

I go to Data Tables.  I select any of my data tables by right clicking on the table's hamburger menu and selecting download, and I don't select saving the n8n fields (id, created at, etc), and the file that gets saved is the same every time (the oldest table)

### Expected behavior

When I select download a csv, the data table I selected should be the data returned.

### Debug Info

no debug info, this is outside of any workflow

### Operating System

Mac

### n8n Version

2.9.3

### Node.js Version

not sure, shouldn't matter

### Database

SQLite (default)

### Execution mode

main (default)

### Hosting

n8n cloud
